title: Citation Map Entry (citation-map v1.0)
description: One JSONL entry of citation_map_jsonl. A citation address pointing at a canonical byte range, optionally linked to a source range and a retrieval chunk. Snapshot metadata is required so a citation can be tied to the exact canonical Markdown used for range and citation-id derivation. Citation Map entries are derived navigation, never canonical content, runtime cache, or content source. The schema validates structure. Ordering constraints such as end_byte > start_byte and end_line >= start_line are semantic validation responsibilities of the future producer/validator. See docs/proofs/citation-map-artifact-fit.md and docs/architecture/range-semantics.md.

citation_id.description: Stable identifier: 'cit_' followed by exactly 16 lower-hex characters. Producers must keep it deterministic across regenerations of the same input. The actual hash derivation is a producer/validator responsibility, not enforced by this schema.

repo_id.description: Repository identifier this citation belongs to.

snapshot.description: Bundle snapshot identity. Required so a citation can be tied to the exact canonical Markdown used for range and citation-id derivation. Allows a consumer to look up the corresponding bundle manifest and validate against changes.

canonical_md_path.description: Path to canonical_md file in the bundle (e.g., 'out.merge.md').

canonical_md_sha256.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical_md file at the time this citation was derived.

canonical_range.description: Position inside canonical_md. Authoritative locator for the citation. content_sha256 hashes the canonical byte slice. Ordering invariants (end_byte > start_byte, end_line >= start_line) are enforced by the producer, not this schema.

file_path.description: Path to canonical_md as referenced by the bundle manifest.

content_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the canonical byte slice [start_byte, end_byte).

source_range.description: Optional position inside the original source file. status is mandatory whenever source_range is present. For status exact, declared, or derived the byte/line fields are required. For status unavailable they may be omitted.

status.description: How trustworthy the source_range is. See range-semantics.md.

chunk_id.description: Optional reference into chunk_index_jsonl. Citation Map does not own chunk identity; it only links to it.

produced_by.description: Optional producer hint, e.g. citation_map_producer name/version. Diagnostic only.

live_repo_address.description: Optional convenience address into the source repository at snapshot time. It is derived navigation only; canonical_range remains the authoritative citation locator.

range_ref.description: Directly resolvable range-ref v1 for the canonical_range. Optional for backwards compatibility with existing citation maps; new producers should emit it.
